steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation for a 45-45-90 triangle
steps.content:
An isosceles right triangle has legs of equal length. Let's call the leg length $a$.

In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse equals $a\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is: $$2a + a\sqrt{2} = a(2 + \sqrt{2}) = 94 + 94\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Test choice B by computing the perimeter
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($47\sqrt{2}$):

If the leg length is $47\sqrt{2}$, the perimeter is: $$47\sqrt{2}(2 + \sqrt{2}) = 47\sqrt{2} \cdot 2 + 47\sqrt{2} \cdot \sqrt{2} = 94\sqrt{2} + 94$$
This equals the given perimeter $94 + 94\sqrt{2}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
